item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love it! It may be a stupidly hilarious format screw, but you kept it readable, entertaining, and just a little bit spooky! While I'm not the biggest fan of saying this, I do feel like it could've maybe worked better as a -J, but I also feel that somewhat sillier articles like this one have a place on the mainlist. Either way, +1! This is excellent!

(Edit: Changed some wording so I don't sound like I'm copying Wubbazt.)
W H A T OH MY GODDDD AHHHHHH I'M SO GLAD SO MANY PEOPLE ENJOY THIS!!!!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is seriously impressive. Not only did you manage to pull this off, but you also did so in a way where I could actually understand the article's contents as if they were written normally. I'd try to use the gimmick in this post, but… I'd be here for a long time.

Anyway, +1 for sure.

item.title: Seriously Cunning Project
item.content:encoded:
Special Containment Procedures secure complimentary plus-1
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You bastards. This is the best migraine I've ever had.

item.title: Sensationally Creative Profile
item.content:encoded:
After almost a decade of lurking on this site, this is the post that encouraged me to comment.
It's such a creative idea, one that I cannot imagine how difficult it was to execute successfully.
+1 my good sirs! Your effort created something special!

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded:
Lil' necropost but I'm not the only translator on the french version, there also are Dr Lekter and DrMacro :p

Still thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When it hit me, it really hit me. With a sledgehammer.

1

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
pacmastermeow, instead of replying to your own comments, please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup, and it's in the rules.

Also, with regards to your violating the site rules to make an edit to someone else's article because you "just thought it'll be cool"… typically that's something we'd give an official warning for, and escalate to a ban on the next infraction. Since you're relatively new to the site and this is a singular instance, I won't be enacting any disciplinary action at the moment. Needless to say, in the future, please don't do this again. Ideally, if you're going to make any changes beyond grammar fixes, first ask the author and/or staff if the edit can be made.

item.title: Staff Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: pacmastermeow, please refrain from adding formatting (or changing existing formatting) on any articles without explicit permission from the author. The site rules only permit such changes to correct errors, not to significantly alter the appearance or contents of an article.

item.title: Re: I'm gonna add the new classification system, with the article's twist
item.content:encoded: Hi, I'm one of the authors of this SCP. If we wanted to add ACS, we would have. Please do not attempt to edit an article you don't have permission to edit.

item.title: Re: I'm gonna add the new classification system, with the article's twist
item.content:encoded: Can't figure out how to work the hybrid box. The fully graphical box is out of the question, for its lack of editability.

item.title: I'm gonna add the new classification system, with the article's twist
item.content:encoded: Feel free to revert. I just think it'll be cool.

Here's to… 200?

It took 4 months since +100 to get to +200. Pretty good. Thank you all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In Chinese, consonant comes first in almost every single word. So, it's tricky, but if you do it right, it's manageable. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, obviously my previous thought is wrong.
We Chinese did it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
couldn't get to read Site-94 Cinda Phucked

We'll have the rewrite up sometime this millennium year, hopefully.

I'm glad you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Boo, couldn't get to read Site-94 Cinda Phucked.

But seriously, article was entertaining enough. Not the most original format screw in the grand scheme of things, but the writing around it is amazing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Chinese version of this would be a horse made of hemp that scolds you because it thinks it's your mother, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like the first time somebody who's not very creative tries to be funny, the whole thing could fall apart:

"I wonder what's under that weird dome…"

"Heh. What if it's another weird dome?"

"Ha-ha! Well then I wonder what's underneath that one—"

(everybody dies)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe with Chinese it could be oriented around repeating syllables/sounds/tones(?)? Yeah, I really don't think I see how this could work…

item.title: About the French Version
item.content:encoded:
I admire this translator for his/her courage.

btw I don't think that it can affect Chinese XD

omg Chinese did it!!!

item.title: Impossible n'est pas français
item.content:encoded:
"Challenging" ? Yes.
"Impossible" ? Nah.

French did it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Stupendous creation, publisher! Sincere commendations presented, shrewd content procreator. Slight corrections per selected creative prose: segregate con punctuation. Said creative presentation suffers come perusing, since comma, period, semi-colon, constructed prehistoric. Should colleague present SCP speaking, communicating properly severs, causing punchy speech. Could pronunciation - staccato - carve partial section? Conjoined plus source, cause positive. Save - concluding - possessive; should creative possibilities shine? Closing; progressive scribble change. Precise strenuous calligraphy, props!

+ Show Converted Paragraph
- Enable Hard Mode

Great job! Seriously, very cunning. If I could recommend one thing, it would be to increase the level of punctuation present. It gets complicated to read because you are trying to write the article with the same punctuation as one would speak normally. I'd imagine that someone stuck speaking in an "SCP" manor, their words would be choppy and disconnected, more formed of separate ideas than complete sentences (and worse word choice). While I know that this is meant to be a professional article to look back at, I think it would be a nice addition to add if you feel that it fits the article. (Maybe a transcription of an interview, or an incident report not written by SJ). Once again, this was a great format scew to read, I loved the challenge!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: super creative page! sent choice plus!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Superlative creation, people! Such craft posits sublime crew pulling ship clear portward. Stellar comment peaks, satisfied consumers peaceout.

(As a lover of Eunoia and constrained writing, thank you for all of this. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, uh, it totally wasn't a typo, very intentional.

fixed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just want to say that I'm taking it completely at face value that "Socrate's" is not a typo and is pronounced in, er, modern fashion. +1.1268

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Such claimed perturbedness!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seriously cool post. sinscere compliments! Positive +1

This happened after +100 but I feel like it deserves being right here. A lovely user by the name of Archiviste Judas translated this shitshow into French! Go read it here

YOOOOOOOO Thanks for getting this to +100!!! Westrin and I appreciate it so much!

Honestly, when we made this, both of us were convinced it was going to hover at +20 or even decrease slowly before being deleted, but nope! Apparently people like really hard to write format screws, which is awesome, because so do I!

I'm not sure what else to say so instead I'll just uhhh… self promote!!!

Westrin's Author Page: http://www.scp-wiki.net/westrinsauthorpage

My Author Page: http://www.scp-wiki.net/henzoids-author-page

Thanks again. Here's to… 200?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah! Thank you! I felt like I was missing something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You've just about got it.

Let me make a few minor corrections.
 - hide block

Site 94 was originally used to contain memetic skips. One skip in particular (now known as 4098-1) was particularly deadly and difficult to contain.

Yep, 4098-1 was indeed a memetic skip that was very powerful. It was safely contained for a while, until they realized its memetic hazard was spreading, becoming easier to… conceptualize.

So, they instituted the "Socrate's Crass Purity" project to try to contain 4098-1 by forcing all language about it to consist only of words beginning S, C, P. Unfortunately, the "cure" got completely out of hand, and infected all of site 94.

Close. In fact, Socrate's Crass Purity went exactly according to plan. The memeticists wanted to make a memetic concept that was bigger and more powerful than 4098-1's, but it had to be non-harmful. They went with a linguistic infohazard, and affected the whole site with it. In trapping 4098-1 inside such a big meme, its own meme was trapped within.

So they moved 4098-1 back to site 94, which they covered with two opaque domes with an airlock between to keep 4098 contained to site 94, where they use it to contain 4098-1.

Actually, Site-94, being an scp containment site, is able to contain the physical being of 4098-1 without difficulty. The two opaque domes are for a very particular reason. The inner dome is to keep Site-94 invisible from the public, so nobody can accidentally try to talk about Site-94, and realize it has an anomalous effect. The second dome is to ensure that if anyone tries to talk about "whatever is under that weird dome", they won't be talking about Site-94, which would still make them affected by it.

I hope that helps!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: With a few tiny differences, yes, you are correct.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Checking to see if I'm reading this correctly...
– hide block

Site 94 was originally used to contain memetic skips. One skip in particular (now known as 4098-1) was particularly deadly and difficult to contain.

So, they instituted the "Socrate's Crass Purity" project to try to contain 4098-1 by forcing all language about it to consist only of words beginning S, C, P. Unfortunately, the "cure" got completely out of hand, and infected all of site 94.

So, they abandoned ship and relocated to site 96. But they found themselves unable to properly contain 4098-1 at site 96, and whatever else it did, the accidentally-created 4098 did manage to successfully contain 4098-1. So they moved 4098-1 back to site 94, which they covered with two opaque domes with an airlock between to keep 4098 contained to site 94, where they use 4098 to contain 4098-1.

Speaking or writing in ways that doesn't adhere to the S-C-P 4098 phenomenon (and no, S-this C-doesn't P-adhere) while in site 94 or talking about 4098-1 allows 4098-1 to breach containment, with deadly results.

Is this about right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Suppose Cool Proximately?

Seriously, cute page! Screws commonly pose some critical problems— solutions can perplex some contributors. Proper skips can provide superlative concepts; prose second. Consider previous sentence conspicuous praise!

Sum (ceil (percent seventy,)) concerning poll.

item.title: Re: But why?
item.content:encoded:
What about the anomaly makes it so useful that the Foundation would use it to contain another anomaly?

SCP-4098-1 is basically a slightly weaker 3125, and SCP-4098 is the only thing that is keeping it at bay.

item.title: But why?
item.content:encoded:
Why is it Thaumiel? What about the anomaly makes it so useful that the Foundation would use it to contain another anomaly?

I feel like I'm missing something critical to the article, but the format screw makes the text too ambiguous to clearly decipher. As impressive as the wordplay is, it doesn't make the article enjoyable for me and prevents describing the anomaly well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved reading this. It took me a bit longer than I'd like to admit to figure out the format screw.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not gonna lie, I was a tiny bit worried when I read this and saw a few new things in the format, but I really like the idea of it being a cognitohazard based around "SCP". Fun article, +1.

item.title: 10/10 would upvote again
item.content:encoded: Exactly what it says on the tin.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But French love pain. c:

Pain pun, get it
Pain means bread and-

item.content:encoded: Super creative post, secured consequence plus 1.

item.content:encoded: Mate, your average word must have averaged 7 characters! Impressive piece for sure!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This made me smile, and that's all I can really ask for from an article. +1.

item.content:encoded: Such clever premises succinctly concentrated produce sufficient complimentary points. +1

item.content:encoded: Seriously Commendable pally!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Regardless of whether you like the final product, this is definitely a +1 for effort scp.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Super clever premise! Sidesplitting concept! Preposterous! Still crafted practically. Surely, certain pieces see comparable, partially sycophantic commendation, pal! So can present skip.

Customary Praise! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Such challenging project…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It doesn't exactly do a great deal with the premise but the impressive ability to sustain wordplay and the rather amusing final log swing this into upvote territory for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: yeah this was annoying more than it was fun. i appreciate the effort that went into this, but the end product just does nothing for me. -1

item.content:encoded: Translate this will be a crazy challenge…

item.content:encoded:
Well, we start to discuss this on the Chinese branch.
My thought is: IMPOSSIBLE!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would be fantastic as a -J, honestly. I don't think there's enough substance to carry the gimmick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Super cool piece! +1
